bullets: Anthropic debuts 'Mythos,' a specialized cybersecurity model deployed via Project Glasswing with Nvidia, Google, AWS, Apple, and Microsoft as partners — the model reportedly identified vulnerabilities across every major OS and browser, signaling that frontier AI is now operationally viable for autonomous defensive security at enterprise scale, and that Anthropic is deliberately building a moat in high-value, high-trust vertical deployments.

Anthropic's annualized revenue run-rate has surged to $30B, prompting a materially expanded compute deal with Google and Broadcom — this confirms that hyperscaler-backed AI incumbents are locking in supply chain advantages at a pace that will be structurally difficult for late movers to match, and that Google's TPU ecosystem is deepening its strategic entanglement with the leading closed-model lab.

Uber is expanding its AWS contract specifically to run workloads on Amazon's custom AI chips (Trainium/Inferentia), an explicit rejection of Oracle and Google as alternatives — this is a meaningful data point that AWS's chip-plus-cloud bundling strategy is converting enterprise AI inference spend in real workloads, not just benchmarks.

Nvidia-backed Firmus hits a $5.5B valuation after raising $1.35B in six months for Asia-Pacific AI data center buildout — the velocity of private capital flowing into GPU-dense infrastructure outside the US hyperscaler orbit underscores that sovereign and regional AI compute capacity is becoming a distinct competitive category OCI should be positioning against.

Intel joins Elon Musk's Terafab US semiconductor project alongside SpaceX and Tesla — while scope is unclear, this signals a credible attempt to establish an alternative domestic chip supply chain that could eventually pressure Nvidia's pricing leverage and reshape the infrastructure cost stack for AI training.
oci_implication_of_day: Uber's explicit move to AWS custom silicon is a direct competitive loss for OCI and a signal that Amazon's vertical integration of chips, cloud, and enterprise contracts is compounding — OCI must accelerate its own differentiated silicon narrative (Arm-based A1, GPU clusters) and ensure its enterprise sales motion can counter AWS bundling before more large-scale inference workloads get locked in. Separately, Anthropic's Project Glasswing partnership list conspicuously excludes OCI; given Oracle's deep government and defense relationships, there is an immediate business development opportunity to position OCI as the sovereign-safe infrastructure layer for AI-driven cybersecurity workloads of exactly this type.
